full_text_rationale: Full text proposes MemCollab, a shared cross-agent memory framework that distills reusable guidance from contrasted trajectories and retrieves it for future inference, with quantitative evaluation on code-generation benchmarks including MBPP and HumanEval.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 011; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Yurui Chang, Yiran Wu, Qingyun Wu, Lu Lin
titulo: MemCollab: Cross-Agent Memory Collaboration via Contrastive Trajectory Distillation
formato_armazenamento: Shared memory bank of contrast-derived normative constraints. Each entry summarizes an enforced reasoning invariant and an avoided violation pattern distilled from preferred and unpreferred trajectories generated by heterogeneous agents on the same task.
framework_agente: MemCollab collaborative memory framework with contrast-derived memory construction and task-aware retrieval
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; cross-agent transfer; cross-model-family transfer; reasoning-turn efficiency; retrieval ablation; case study
metricas_desempenho: accuracy, average score, average reasoning turns, retrieval-count sensitivity, retrieval-strategy comparison
metricas_cl: Contrast-derived memory entries, shared agent-agnostic memory bank, task-category labels, top-p task-aware retrieval, comparison with self-memory and direct cross-model memory transfer
baseline_comparado: Vanilla agent, Buffer of Thoughts, Dynamic Cheatsheet, memory from same model, memory from another model, self-contrast memory
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Each task contributes up to K extracted memory entries; inference retrieves top-3 relevant samples within task-filtered subsets, with each retrieved sample contributing up to three memory entries.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; experiments report reasoning-turn reduction and use 8 NVIDIA RTX 6000 GPUs with CUDA 12.8, but no monetary cost.
resultado_principal: MemCollab improves both weaker and stronger agents by constructing shared memory from contrastive trajectories rather than directly transferring model-specific memory.
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Qwen2.5-7B, MemCollab improves average accuracy from 57.1 to 71.6; MBPP improves from 47.9 to 57.6 and HumanEval from 42.7 to 74.4. For Qwen2.5-32B, average accuracy improves from 70.8 to 79.6.
custo_vs_baseline: MemCollab reduces average reasoning turns for Qwen2.5-7B: MATH500 2.7 to 2.2, GSM8K 1.8 to 1.6, MBPP 3.1 to 1.4, and HumanEval 3.3 to 1.5.
limitacoes: Direct memory transfer between models can degrade performance; too many retrieved memory entries can reduce performance after a task-dependent threshold; evaluation spans math and code benchmarks rather than full software-engineering workflows.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on task classification, TF-IDF retrieval, top-3 memory budget, paired-agent choice, benchmark sample construction, random seed, and whether contrastive summaries suppress model-specific biases without dropping useful details.
